key_insight: This pilot trial tests whether a resilience-focused, virtual physical activity program can help adolescents with chronic musculoskeletal pain stay active despite pain-related barriers.
clinical_relevance: If feasible and acceptable, PREP-Y could inform a future randomized study aimed at improving function, activity, and coping in teens with chronic pain.
method: 40 adolescents aged 12–17 with chronic musculoskeletal pain
4 virtual intervention sessions targeting self-efficacy, motivation, coping, and pain resilience
Baseline, post-intervention, and 3-month follow-up with questionnaires, physical tests, and activity monitoring
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a pilot phase 2 single-group trial focused on feasibility and acceptability, with exploratory measures of activity, pain-related functioning, and resilience constructs rather than efficacy outcomes.
watch_item: Because this is a small, non-randomized single-site pilot, results may not generalize and cannot establish causal effectiveness.
paper_title: Resilience factors, pain, and physical activity in adolescent chronic musculoskeletal pain: design and protocol of a pilot phase 2 single-group, non-randomized clinical trial
